words.def: a situation or a pattern of behaviour that is usual or expected
words.example: The new design is a departure from the norm.

words.def: a small book of plain paper for writing notes in
words.example: The police officer wrote the details down in his notebook.

words.def: an idea, a belief or an understanding of something
words.example: notion of something, a political system based on the notions of equality and liberty

words.def: a person who writes novels
words.example: a romantic/historical novelist

words.def: at the present time, in contrast with the past
words.example: Nowadays most kids prefer going online to reading books.

words.def: existing in large numbers
words.example: He has been late on numerous occasions.

words.def: the job or skill of caring for people who are sick or injured
words.example: a career in nursing

words.def: a small hard fruit with a very hard shell that grows on some trees
words.example: to crack a nut (= open it)

words.def: the process by which living things receive the food necessary for them to grow and be healthy
words.example: advice on diet and nutrition

words.def: the quality or fact of being very fat, in a way that is not healthy
words.example: Obesity can increase the risk of heart disease.

words.def: to do what you are told or expected to do
words.example: obey something, to obey a command/an order/rules/the law

words.def: a thing that can be seen and touched, but is not alive
words.example: everyday objects such as cups and saucers

words.def: not influenced by personal feelings or opinions; considering only facts
words.example: an objective assessment of the situation

words.def: the state of being forced to do something because it is your duty, or because of a law, etc.
words.example: obligation to do something, You are under no obligation to buy anything.

words.def: the act of watching somebody/something carefully for a period of time, especially to learn something
words.example: Most information was collected by direct observation of the animals' behaviour.

words.def: to see or notice somebody/something
words.example: observe somebody/something, Have you observed any changes lately?

words.def: a person who watches somebody/something
words.example: According to observers, the plane exploded shortly after take-off.

words.def: a situation, an event, etc. that makes it difficult for you to do or achieve something
words.example: So far, we have managed to overcome all the obstacles that have been placed in our path.

words.def: to get something, especially by making an effort
words.example: to obtain information/data/results

words.def: sometimes but not often
words.example: We occasionally meet for a drink after work.

words.def: a job or profession
words.example: Please state your name, age and occupation below.

words.def: to fill or use a space, an area or an amount of time
words.example: The bed seemed to occupy most of the room.

words.def: strange or unusual
words.example: They're very odd people.

words.def: an illegal act
words.example: a criminal/serious/minor/sexual offence

words.def: to make somebody feel upset because of something you say or do that is rude or embarrassing
words.example: They'll be offended if you don't go to their wedding.

words.def: a person who commits a crime
words.example: a persistent/serious/violent, etc. offender

words.def: rude in a way that causes somebody to feel upset or annoyed because it shows a lack of respect
words.example: offensive remarks

words.def: agreed to, said, done, etc. by somebody who is in a position of authority
words.example: an official announcement/decision/statement

words.def: continuing to exist or develop
words.example: an ongoing debate/discussion/process

words.def: a space or hole that somebody/something can pass through
words.example: We could see the stars through an opening in the roof.

words.def: without hiding any feelings, opinions or information
words.example: Can you talk openly about sex with your parents?

words.def: a dramatic work in which all or most of the words are sung to music; works of this type as an art form or entertainment
words.example: Puccini’s operas

words.def: to work in a particular way
words.example: Most domestic freezers operate at below −18°C.

words.def: a person who operates equipment or a machine
words.example: a computer/machine operator

words.def: a person that you are playing or fighting against in a game, competition, argument, etc.
words.example: a political opponent

words.def: to disagree strongly with somebody’s plan, policy, etc. and try to change it or prevent it from succeeding
words.example: oppose somebody/something, This party would bitterly oppose the re-introduction of the death penalty.

words.def: disagreeing strongly with something and trying to stop it
words.example: opposed to something, He was strongly opposed to modernism in art.

words.def: the act of strongly disagreeing with somebody/something, especially with the aim of preventing something from happening
words.example: Opposition came primarily from students.

words.def: expecting good things to happen or something to be successful; showing this feeling
words.example: We are now taking a more optimistic view.

words.def: a large group of people who play various musical instruments together, led by a conductor
words.example: She plays the flute in the school orchestra.

words.def: a part of the body that has a particular purpose, such as the heart or the brain; part of a plant with a particular purpose
words.example: the internal organs

words.def: produced or practised without using artificial chemicals
words.example: organic cheese/vegetables/wine, etc.

words.def: a person who makes the arrangements for something
words.example: the organizers of the festival

words.def: the point from which something starts; the cause of something
words.example: origin of something, the origins of life on earth

words.def: used to describe the situation that existed at the beginning of a particular period or activity, especially before something was changed
words.example: The school was originally very small.

words.def: used to state what the result would be if something did not happen or if the situation were different
words.example: My parents lent me the money. Otherwise, I couldn't have afforded the trip.

words.def: the result or effect of an action or event
words.example: The likely outcome is a compromise.

words.def: on the outside of something
words.example: the outer layers of the skin

words.def: a set of clothes that you wear together, especially for a particular occasion or purpose
words.example: She was wearing an expensive new outfit.

words.def: a description of the main facts or points involved in something
words.example: outline of something, This is a brief outline of the events.

words.def: the amount of something that a person, a machine or an organization produces
words.example: Manufacturing output has increased by 8 per cent.

words.def: of, on or facing the outer side
words.example: The outside walls are damp.

words.def: extremely good; excellent
words.example: an outstanding player/achievement/success

words.def: including all the things or people that are involved in a particular situation; general
words.example: There will be winners in each of three age groups, and one overall winner.

words.def: to succeed in dealing with or controlling a problem that has been preventing you from achieving something
words.example: She overcame injury to win the Olympic gold medal.

words.def: during or for the night
words.example: We stayed overnight in London after the theatre.

words.def: connected with foreign countries, especially those separated from your country by the sea or ocean
words.example: The firm is expanding into overseas markets.

words.def: to have to pay somebody for something that you have already received or return money that you have borrowed
words.example: owe somebody something, She still owes her father £3 000.
